topSignals.text: GPT-5 context window is now 1M tokens in the API for all paid tiers. Writing code that touches your entire codebase in one call is now real. Pricing stays the same.
topSignals.summary: GPT-5 gets a 1M-token context window across all paid API tiers at unchanged pricing.
topSignals.whyItMatters: 1M context at current price points fundamentally changes what's practical for code and document analysis.

topSignals.text: OpenAI Operator mode can now fill forms, book appointments, and navigate any website in the browser. It asks before consequential actions. This is the beginning of the agent layer.
topSignals.summary: OpenAI Operator ships browser-level agency including form filling, booking, and navigation with consent checkpoints.
topSignals.whyItMatters: Browser-native agents with consent gates marks the practical start of the agentic automation era.

topSignals.text: Gemini 2.5 Pro scored above human expert level on 12 new graduate-level chemistry benchmarks. This is a meaningful science milestone — not just a benchmark number.
topSignals.summary: Gemini 2.5 Pro achieves above-human-expert performance on 12 graduate-level chemistry benchmarks.
topSignals.whyItMatters: Scientific domain competence at expert level has direct implications for drug discovery and materials research.

topSignals.text: o3-mini is now available in the API. 40% cheaper than o3, same reasoning quality on most coding and math benchmarks. Rate limits are 2x higher at launch.
topSignals.summary: o3-mini launches in the API at 40% lower cost than o3 with doubled rate limits.
topSignals.whyItMatters: Cost reduction on the best reasoning model makes it practical for higher-volume applications.

topSignals.text: Built an agent that generates, tests, and deploys microservices end-to-end from a single prompt. Not perfect, but ships working code 70% of the time on first try. Demo thread.
topSignals.summary: Demonstrates an agent that autonomously generates, tests, and deploys microservices from a single prompt at 70% success rate.
topSignals.whyItMatters: Agentic code deployment hitting 70% reliability on first try is a meaningful practical threshold.

activePeople.currentFocus: Posting API updates, new model launches, and developer-facing product changes at OpenAI.
Posting about AI-assisted development workflows, primarily Swift/iOS, with Claude Code usage.
Publishing weekly AI paper digests and open-source LLM leaderboard updates.
Publishing prompt engineering patterns focused on information density and reliability.
Posting MRR updates and shipping velocity insights from building multiple AI products solo.
Benchmarking open-source LLMs and publishing practical fine-tuning tutorials.

latestFeed.text: nanoGPT v2 is out. Added flash attention, gradient checkpointing, and a cleaner training loop. ~40% faster on A100.
latestFeed.summary: nanoGPT v2 ships with flash attention and 40% training speed improvement on A100.

latestFeed.text: o3-mini is now available in the API. 40% cheaper than o3, same reasoning quality on most coding and math benchmarks.
latestFeed.summary: o3-mini launches in the API at 40% lower cost than o3 with doubled rate limits.

latestFeed.text: Built an agent that generates, tests, and deploys microservices end-to-end from a single prompt.
latestFeed.summary: Demonstrates an agent that autonomously generates, tests, and deploys microservices at 70% success rate.

latestFeed.text: Chain-of-density prompting: get 5x more information density in AI summaries through iterative compression.
latestFeed.summary: Publishes a chain-of-density prompting guide for higher-information summaries.

latestFeed.text: Benchmarked 8 open models on multi-step instruction following and structured JSON output tasks.
latestFeed.summary: Detailed benchmark comparing 8 open models on instruction-following and structured output tasks.
